LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GLOBAL COLLECT USA, INC., INGENICO, INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANTS GLOBAL COLLECT USA, INC. AND INGENICO INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF FREDERIK MARTIN TOBER'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND BREACH OF BUSINESS & PROFESSIONS CODE SECTION 17200, AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND, THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INTERFERENCE WITH CONTRACTUAL RELATIONS, WILLFUL FAILURE TO PAY WAGES IN VIOLATION OF THE LABOR CODE, AND INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. Defendants Global Collect Usa, Inc., Ingenico, Inc.'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint (Added For Purposes Of Entering Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Feb-20-2019 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Brian Bercovitz, Esq., Weems Law Offics, Telephone 415-881-7653, appearing for Plaintiff;
Aaron Langberg, esq., Fisher Phillips, Telephone 415-490-9014, appearing for Defendant(s) Global Collect Services USA Inc, et al;

Ruling - ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GLOBAL COLLECT USA, INC., INGENICO, INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Defendants Global Collect USA, Inc. and Ingenico Inc.'s demurrer to Plaintiff Frederik Martin Tober's first amended complaint is overruled as to the first and fifth causes of action for breach of contract and breach of Business & Professions Code section 17200, and sustained without leave to amend as to the second, third and fourth causes of action for interference with contractual relations, willful failure to pay wages in violation of the Labor Code, and intentional interference with prospective economic advantage. Counsel for Defendant to submit form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312.

(See word minutes for complete details)
